mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative mean 60-day forward returns or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually, concurrent increases in the federal funds rate signal aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.01 during GDP expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 positions indicates sudden labor market contraction and weakening economic activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of instances where the payroll decline threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales demonstrate synchronized supply and demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes yield negative mean 5-day forward returns or fail to beat a buy-and-hold baseline.
